questions.q: Prithviraj Chauhan belonged to which Rajput clan?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj III was a king of the Chahamana, anglicised as Chauhan, dynasty.

questions.q: In which century CE did Prithviraj Chauhan rule?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj Chauhan reigned in the late 12th century CE.

questions.q: Prithviraj Chauhan ruled from Ajmer and which other major city?
questions.explanation: His kingdom was centred on Ajmer and Delhi in northern India.

questions.q: Prithviraj Chauhan is popularly remembered as the last Hindu emperor of which city?
questions.explanation: He is celebrated in tradition as the last Hindu ruler of Delhi before Turkish rule.

questions.q: Which adversary did Prithviraj Chauhan fight at the Battles of Tarain?
questions.explanation: His famous battles were fought against the Ghurid ruler Muhammad of Ghor.

questions.q: At the First Battle of Tarain, what was the outcome for Prithviraj Chauhan?
options: He won and Muhammad of Ghor retreated
questions.explanation: Prithviraj defeated Muhammad of Ghor at the First Battle of Tarain in 1191 CE.

questions.q: What happened to Prithviraj Chauhan after the Second Battle of Tarain?
questions.explanation: He was defeated at the Second Battle of Tarain in 1192 CE and subsequently killed.

questions.q: An epic poem celebrating Prithviraj Chauhan's life is known as the?
questions.explanation: The Prithviraj Raso, attributed to the poet Chand Bardai, narrates his legendary deeds.

questions.q: The poet traditionally credited as Prithviraj Chauhan's court bard was?
questions.explanation: Chand Bardai is traditionally named as Prithviraj's court poet and author of the Prithviraj Raso.

questions.q: Prithviraj Chauhan's resistance came against invaders advancing from which direction?
questions.explanation: The Ghurid armies advanced into India from the northwest, through Afghanistan and the Punjab.

questions.q: Prithviraj Chauhan is also referred to by which regnal numbering?
questions.explanation: He is known to historians as Prithviraj III of the Chahamana line.

questions.q: Which earlier Chahamana ruler, an uncle of Prithviraj's father's line, had expanded the kingdom and taken Delhi?
questions.explanation: Vigraharaja IV (Visaladeva), a powerful Chahamana king who took Delhi, was a notable predecessor in Prithviraj's family line.

questions.q: According to popular legend, Prithviraj Chauhan eloped with the daughter of which rival king?
questions.explanation: Legend tells of his abduction of Sanyogita, daughter of the Gahadavala king Jaichand of Kannauj.

questions.q: Muhammad of Ghor, Prithviraj's foe, was based in which region?
questions.explanation: Muhammad of Ghor ruled from Ghor in the highlands of present-day Afghanistan.

questions.q: Prithviraj Chauhan's capital fortress in his ancestral domain was at?
questions.explanation: Ajmer, founded as Ajayameru by the Chahamanas, was the dynasty's ancestral capital.

questions.q: The Battles of Tarain were fought near a site in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Tarain (modern Taraori) lies in present-day Haryana, north of Delhi.

questions.q: Prithviraj Chauhan's defeat ultimately paved the way for the founding of which state?
questions.explanation: The Ghurid victory opened the way for the Delhi Sultanate, founded by Aibak in 1206.

questions.q: Before clashing with the Ghurids, Prithviraj Chauhan also waged war against which neighbouring Hindu kingdom?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj campaigned against the Chandelas, capturing Mahoba, among other rivals.

questions.q: The legend of Prithviraj Chauhan emphasises his rivalry with which fellow Rajput king, weakening Hindu unity?
questions.explanation: His feud with Jaichand of Kannauj is traditionally cited as undermining a united defence against the Ghurids.

questions.q: The Prithviraj Raso, though celebrated, is regarded by historians as largely what?
options: A later embellished legend rather than reliable history
questions.explanation: Scholars treat the Raso as a much later bardic epic blending legend with history, not a contemporary source.

questions.q: After his defeat, Prithviraj's kingdom of Ajmer was initially handed by the Ghurids to whom?
options: His son, as a tributary vassal
questions.explanation: The Ghurids briefly installed Prithviraj's son as a vassal ruler of Ajmer before tightening direct control.

questions.q: Prithviraj Chauhan is celebrated chiefly as a symbol of what?
questions.explanation: He endures in popular memory as an icon of Rajput martial valour and resistance to invasion.

questions.q: Prithviraj Chauhan ascended the throne at a young age, with his early administration partly guided by which minister?
questions.explanation: Court records indicate the minister Kadambavasa, also called Kaimasa, helped administer the early reign.

questions.q: In which years were the First and Second Battles of Tarain fought, respectively?
questions.explanation: The First Battle was in 1191 CE (Prithviraj's victory) and the Second in 1192 CE (his defeat).

questions.q: Before Tarain, Prithviraj defeated the Chandela ruler Paramardideva and captured which fort city?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj's campaign against the Chandelas led to the capture of Mahoba around 1182 CE.

questions.q: Contemporary or near-contemporary works on Prithviraj include which Sanskrit text by Jayanaka?
questions.explanation: The Prithviraja Vijaya by Jayanaka is a near-contemporary Sanskrit account, more reliable than the later Raso.

questions.q: The legend that a blinded Prithviraj killed Muhammad of Ghor with a 'shabdabhedi' arrow shot appears in which work?
questions.explanation: This dramatic but historically doubtful episode is told in the bardic Prithviraj Raso.

questions.q: Historically, the more credible accounts agree that after Tarain Prithviraj was what?
options: Sent into comfortable exile in Persia
Retired to a monastery in Tibet
Captured and put to death by the Ghurids
questions.explanation: Sources such as Minhaj-i-Siraj's Tabaqat-i-Nasiri indicate Prithviraj was captured and executed after the Ghurid victory, not freed; the bardic 'blind archer' survival tale is legend.

questions.q: The Chahamanas of Prithviraj's branch are specifically known as the Chahamanas of which place?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj belonged to the Chahamanas of Shakambhari (Sambhar), the senior Chauhan line based at Ajmer.

questions.q: Muhammad of Ghor's general who consolidated the conquest of Delhi after Prithviraj's fall was?
questions.explanation: Qutb-ud-din Aibak, a slave-general of Muhammad of Ghor, secured Delhi and later founded the Slave dynasty.
